summary: Alfred is a terminal UI (TUI) editor, not a web application. It produces no HTML, CSS, or web content. WCAG 2.2 accessibility criteria are not applicable. The codebase uses ratatui for terminal rendering and crossterm for terminal control. Terminal accessibility is governed by different standards (e.g., ANSI compatibility, screen reader support from terminal emulator). This assessment is not applicable to this codebase.
assessment_notes: Alfred is a terminal-based Emacs-like text editor. The codebase renders to a terminal via ratatui (TUI framework) and crossterm (cross-platform terminal control). There are no HTML, CSS, JavaScript, or web UI files. The rendering target is the terminal emulator, not a web browser. Terminal accessibility depends on: (1) terminal emulator capabilities (e.g., xterm, iTerm2, Windows Terminal), (2) screen reader support in the terminal environment, (3) ANSI color and text styling support. WCAG 2.2 applies to web content and web applications, not terminal interfaces. Instead, terminal accessibility is governed by: - ANSI/ECMA-48 standard for terminal control sequences - Terminal emulator support for alt-text, color contrast, keyboard navigation - Screen reader integration at the OS level (e.g., NVDA on Windows, VoiceOver on macOS) - Keyboard-only operation (which Alfred supports via vim-keybindings plugin). Terminal editors typically fall under a different accessibility framework than web UIs.

recommendations.title: Evaluate terminal accessibility separately from WCAG
recommendations.description: Conduct terminal accessibility assessment using terminal-specific standards: ANSI compliance, screen reader integration testing (NVDA, JAWS, VoiceOver), keyboard-only operation verification, color contrast in terminal rendering.

recommendations.title: Ensure color contrast in ratatui rendering
recommendations.description: Review terminal color themes for adequate contrast ratios between foreground and background colors in syntax highlighting and UI elements. Test with low-vision color simulators.

recommendations.title: Verify keyboard navigation coverage
recommendations.description: Alfred already supports vim-keybindings for navigation. Ensure all editor commands are keyboard-accessible without requiring mouse or graphical UI. Document keyboard shortcuts.

recommendations.title: Test with screen readers in terminal environments
recommendations.description: Use NVDA (Windows), JAWS (Windows), or VoiceOver (macOS) to test if terminal rendering is readable by screen readers. This requires terminal emulator support, which varies.

recommendations.title: Document terminal accessibility assumptions
recommendations.description: Create a TERMINAL_ACCESSIBILITY.md documenting which terminal emulators are tested, screen reader compatibility, color contrast values, and keyboard navigation model.
